intro: Microchilus longibracteatus, commonly known as the "Long-bracted Microchile", is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, M. longibracteatus has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its reported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of M. longibracteatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, M. longibracteatus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas, capsules, and ointments to harness its potential health benefits.
